value.reason: The page does document this capability — the glossary entry "Auto memory" describes Claude writing notes to a MEMORY.md file per git repository, stored under ~/.claude/projects/, with the first 200 lines/25 KB loading at session start. However, the cell label "MEMORY.md" is inaccurate: the provider's real name for this feature is "Auto memory" (as explicitly labeled in the glossary). The description is also partially inaccurate: it says the file is stored "per git repository" and implies it's stored at the repository root, but the page states it's stored under ~/.claude/projects/ (not in the repo itself). The description also says "without manual intervention" and frames MEMORY.md as the sole mechanism, whereas the page clarifies it is the Claude-written counterpart to the user-written CLAUDE.md. The core functional claim (Claude writes notes based on corrections/preferences, file auto-loads at session start) is broadly correct, but the label is wrong (should be "Auto memory") and the storage location detail is misleading.
